other_observers: Brad Steger  Mark Peterson; Brad Steger found the birds
description: These were large white aquatic birds with longish arched necks, i.e. swans. The bills were black with yellowish lore extending onto bill. Body gray white with humped back. Legs blackish, feet not seen. Bill with rounded upper edge between eyes. Eye dark. Head rounded and neck noticeably shorter than that found on Trumpter Swan. Necks often bent in "S" curve while swimming. I looked for the suggested curved sides to the bill when in profile which was present but not noticeably different than the Pueblo TRSW. The head appeared quite rounded. The bill shape was not like that portrayed by Canvasback and therefore TRSW. The center of the bill was slightly concave and the bill seems shorter than what I remembered from the Pueblo TRSW.Behaviors: Searched for food in shallow water but I did not see them find any food. The never tipped completely butt up to feed, but instead kept their whole head and only a small amount of neck under water. Three swans stayed quite close all of the time and near the fourth all of the time. When the group moved towards or away from shore all members of group did the same. Often seen near a roosting flock of Ring-billed Gulls and searched for food with three species of dabling duck. Did not seem too nervous when a jogger went by, but again the distance was significant, albeit totally open.Call: none heardPlumage: adult basic
